48 - 9 Minute Training To Destroy Any Sales Objection - YouTube.mp4
How do you get your prospects to persuade themselves? I don't have the money. Will it work for me? I'm not sure I should do it. Next year is better. I'll do it down the road. I need to think it over. I need to do it. research. How much is it going to cost? These are frames that your prospects have. And until you learn how to get them out of their frame, what are they going to do? They're going to give you these objections over and over and over and over again. And I just don't like getting objections. It's just not fun. Anybody ever get a call or worse, an email, the email death. Oh, we really liked you and what you had to say, but. Now's not a good time for us.
We'll get back to you when we're interested. Keep in touch. What are you going to do? What are you going to do now? You're going to call them back, thrust them rebuttals, tell them why they're wrong. How often does that work? Oh, I mean, it works every once in a while. Numbers game, right? I don't want you to have to play that game. It's a really rough one. Not very fun. Okay, here's what we're going to do. Somebody be the prospect. You'd be the prospect? Yeah, what do you sell? Health consulting. Health consulting for what? People who have low energy. Oh, like a coaching program. Yeah. Like for entrepreneurs or something, or just anybody. Mainly entrepreneurs, low energy, brain fog, poor focus. And what are the consequences if they don't purchase your program?
They won't have enough energy to dominate the business. We'll lose a lot of money and a lot of productivity. Okay, and if they lose a lot of money in productivity, what's the consequences? Like, what happens then? Their business won't make as much money and won't perform as well. And what happens to the business then? When it doesn't perform. Probably won't be around much longer. And what happens then when it goes out of business? They'll probably be busking outside the casino. No, seriously. On a serious note, what happens to the business owner when they go under? Not good things. Like what? Tell me. They'd be unemployed. Okay. And they're not making any money. What happens to their family? Can't provide for the family. Yeah. It's much deeper than just... Fixing their health.
Yeah. See how I could go six layers deeper than that. That's where the sale is made. Not up here. About getting healthier. Yeah. It's the consequences of not, of what can happen if they don't. It's the fear of getting them to feel and see. What? The future could look like if they don't solve that. It's a pain of fear. What are the two biggest emotional drivers that cause a human being to want to change? Pain? And the fear of future pain. So, if you can't help them relive their pain of their current situation, their past, and then have a fear of future pain, they feel no need to what? Change. There's that word change again. And if your prospect feels no need to change, that's why they...
give you objections, and that's why they don't buy from you. Even though they basically raise your hand saying 'help.' Do you have book calls? They book on your calendar? Yep. Without you getting upset with me. That concern tone seeds what? Doubt. That I know something he doesn't know and that triggers doubt in his brain where his guard comes down and he becomes more open. Identity frame. Well, for you, why look at doing this now? Why not push it down the road like a lot of executives who never end up getting promoted because they never get their energy and health back? That's an identity frame. See how I'm getting him to run away from that type of executive that has negative, negative, negative. Does he want to be like that executive? See, I'm getting him to push back against that. That's called an identity frame. I'm getting him out of his current way of thinking, push it down the road to not identify with all those other people out there that keep having that problem. Does that make sense what I just did there?
